skill.name: claude-win11-speckit-update-skill
skill.description: Provides high-level guidance for Windows 11 system management tasks.
skill.summary: Guides AI assistants through Windows 11 system management requests.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found two hardcoded repository URLs and two weak-cryptography indicators. The URL findings are passive documentation links, and the cryptography findings are false positives on plain descriptive text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 4 stores the source repository URL in metadata. It does not fetch data or send information anywhere.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter metadata with a GitHub repository URL. No executable network request or data transfer is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 22 links to the source repository for documentation. It is a reference link, not active network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is Markdown documentation linking to GitHub. No command, script, or automatic request uses the URL.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 3 contains the skill description text. It does not reference or invoke a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain prose about Windows 11 system management. No cryptographic API, hash, cipher, or implementation appears there.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Line 20 contains general guidance text. It does not contain cryptographic logic or algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a natural-language instruction sentence. There is no evidence of weak cryptography or security-sensitive code.

content.user_title: Manage Windows 11 Tasks with AI Guidance
content.value_statement: Windows 11 system tasks can be hard to frame clearly for an AI assistant. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused context for system management guidance.
actual_capabilities: Identifies Windows 11 system management as the intended task area.
Provides a simple usage trigger for Windows 11 management requests.
Supplies high-level guidance and patterns for AI-assisted system management.
Points users to the public source repository for more project context.
Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as a lightweight instruction skill.
limitations: Does not include scripts, commands, or automated Windows configuration steps.
Does not validate system state or inspect a Windows machine directly.
Does not provide detailed troubleshooting procedures inside the skill file.
Requires the user to supply task details, constraints, and environment context.

use_cases.title: Plan a Windows 11 Maintenance Task
use_cases.description: Create a clear request for routine system management work before making changes.

use_cases.title: Frame Support Guidance
use_cases.description: Help support staff turn a broad Windows 11 issue into structured next steps.

use_cases.title: Review Management Approach
use_cases.description: Ask an AI assistant to review a proposed Windows 11 management workflow for gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Describe a Basic Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for simple Windows 11 guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help me describe a Windows 11 system management task. Ask clarifying questions before suggesting steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Troubleshooting Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for an organized support flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline a Windows 11 troubleshooting plan for this issue: [describe issue]. Include checks, risks, and rollback notes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Proposed Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request before applying a system change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review this Windows 11 management plan: [paste plan]. Identify assumptions, missing safeguards, and safer sequencing.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Admin Runbook
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for repeatable operational guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a Windows 11 system management runbook for [task]. Include prerequisites, verification steps, and escalation criteria.

output_examples.input: Help me plan a Windows 11 system cleanup task.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about device ownership, backups, target cleanup areas, and acceptable downtime before suggesting a cautious task sequence.

output_examples.input: Review my Windows 11 update workflow before I run it.
output_examples.output: The assistant highlights missing prerequisites, recommends verification points, and suggests rollback notes for the update workflow.

output_examples.input: Create support notes for a Windows 11 issue.
output_examples.output: The assistant produces a structured support outline with symptoms, checks, likely causes, next actions, and escalation triggers.

best_practices: State the Windows 11 edition, device role, and user impact before asking for guidance.
Ask for verification and rollback steps before applying system changes.
Treat generated guidance as planning support and confirm actions against official documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad system changes without describing the target device and constraints.
Do not run suggested commands without reviewing their effect and rollback path.
Do not use this skill as a replacement for endpoint management policy or vendor guidance.

faq.question: Does this skill run Windows commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill file contains guidance only and does not include executable commands or scripts.

faq.question: Can it inspect my Windows 11 computer?
faq.answer: No. It cannot access device state unless you provide details in your prompt.

faq.question: Which assistants can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this an official Microsoft tool?
faq.answer: No. It is a community skill that references a public GitHub source repository.

faq.question: Why is there a network risk factor?
faq.answer: The skill includes GitHub URLs as documentation references. They are not active network requests.

faq.question: What information should I include in prompts?
faq.answer: Include the Windows 11 version, device type, goal, constraints, and any required change controls.
